urn:contentItem:6JHJ-4M53-RXNT-64MB-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ U.S. ARMY CORPS FUNDS $610M FACILITY TO PRODUCE STERILE SCREWWORMS; MORTENSON TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION

M A Mortenson Company has won a $610 million contract from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to construct the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas. The bid supports ongoing efforts to prevent the New World screwworm from spreading into the United States from Mexico, where authorities report rising activity near the Texas border. As of March 4, 2026, there were 989 active cases nationwide, including 19 in Tamaulipas, a rise from 11 weeks prior, underscoring the need for robust control measures such as the deployment of sterilized flies along the border. Mortenson’s facility will play a pivotal role in ensuring a reliable supply of sterile insects, reinforcing the USDA’s efforts to protect livestock and sustain farm income in border states.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO HEAD OF CAPITAL STRATEGY

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, based in the firm's New York City office.He will refine and execute the funding strategy, reporting to president and CEO James Patchett, and will help guide capital strategy and investment decisions while building relationships with funding and equity partners.Vlahakis previously served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he led equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities highlights his breadth of financial leadership.Gilbane says the appointment strengthens its capital program and supports its growth plans in core sectors.

+ GILBANE-WKU PPP AIMS TO MODERNIZE HOUSING, EXPAND STUDENT RESOURCES

WKU announced the approval of a public-private partnership with Gilbane to develop a new residence hall replacing Keen and Poland Halls, with the Board of Regents' approval on 11th December 2025. The Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026 clears the way for construction. SGA Senator Barker stated the partnership is fully authorized and that WKU is now ready to begin building. The SGA also advanced measures to benefit students, including Resolution 7-26-S for a crosswalk near housing and collaboration with the Kentucky DOT, and Bill 17-26-S providing $50 reimbursements for textbooks, along with provisions for Scantrons and lab goggles.

+ DC AFFORDABLE HOUSING EXPANSION: GILBANE STARTS LEASING FOR 229-UNIT PHASE II

Gilbane Development has initiated leasing for Phase II of its Barnaby & 7th redevelopment in Southeast Washington, D.C., delivering 229 units across four new buildings, The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis. As of 3rd April 2026, the project is on track for completion by summer 2026 and features a mix of studios through three-bedroom units serving households at 50% and 80% of AMI, with 20% of units set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing under DC DHS.Phase I, The Upland, was completed in Spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, including 34 PSH units. Income restrictions for Phase I range from 30% to 50% of AMI.Phase III is expected to add about 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the grand total to 498 units upon completion. A pedestrian walkway now connects all six buildings in the larger development, and DC Housing Finance Agency places Phase II's cost at roughly $135 million.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-M503-S6WK-11FP-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

value.Title: J-Star Holding Joins Forces with Patriot Green Energy Technology for Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-4723-RSJV-W30B-00000-00J-Star Holding Joins Forces with Patriot Green Energy Technology for Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisJ-Star Holding Joins Forces with Patriot Green Energy Technology for Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions

J-Star Holding Co., Ltd., reported it has formed a strategic partnership with Patriot Green Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (PSSB), an industry developer and manufacturer of next-generation solid state battery technology for high-performance and defense applications with a large minority ownership by Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI), to develop Made-in-USA polymer-based solid-state battery solutions.

The company said the collaboration includes plans to establish a 100 megawatt-hour (MWh) modular, automated solid-state battery production line in Baytown, Texas to support the growing requirements of the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) and drone sector.

In a release, J-Star Holding noted partnership details and highlights include:

J-Star and PSSB have jointly submitted a grant application to the U.S. Department of Energy to support the development of the facility. The project has also received a commitment letter from the Baytown Economic Development Foundation securing a parcel in the TGS Cedar Port Industrial Park for construction of the factory.

The initiative is aligned with the U.S.-Taiwan Agreement on Research and Technology (ART) framework, which provides a bilateral pathway for technical cooperation and outward investment clearances from Taiwan's Ministry of Economic Affairs (MOEA). In January 2026, the United States and Taiwan signed a landmark trade and investment agreement with Taiwanese semiconductor and technology companies committing $250 billion in new direct investment into the United States and $250 billion in credit guarantee by the government of Taiwan to support additional investment, strengthening collaboration in semiconductor supply chains, industrial parks, artificial intelligence, energy, and defense technologies.

Jonathan Chiang, CEO and Chairman of J-Star Holding, commented, "We continue to strengthen our relationship with PSSB with this new 'Made-In-USA' initiative to develop a fully automated next-generation solid-state batteries production line in Texas. This partnership marks a major step in building a secure, friend-shored supply chain infrastructure and solutions in the United States. With strong government support and growing demand from aerospace and defense customers, we believe this positions J-Star at the forefront of advance battery manufacturing."

Cheng Zhang Lu (Peter Lu), Chairman & CEO of PSSB, has committed to transferring the company's full technical blueprint to the Baytown facility, including extended on-site technical residency, local hiring, and workforce training to support long-term operations. This partnership combines ITRI-backed innovation with U.S. manufacturing capabilities to accelerate the commercialization of polymer-based solid-state batteries and help establish a domestic production ecosystem for the rapidly growing UAV and drone markets.

Strategic Partnership and Market Opportunity

Through YMA (TX) INC., J-Star is leveraging PSSB's venture backing from ITRI to pursue supply agreements with Tier-1 U.S. aerospace and defense OEMs. The partnership is designed to accelerate qualification with U.S. federal contractors by leveraging technology that has already undergone validation in Taiwan.

The project aims to establish a U.S. domestic, fully automated production line for high-density polymer-based solid-state batteries supporting the rapidly expanding UAV and drone markets. The facility is expected to scale manufacturing to 100 MWh annual nameplate capacity, targeting over 350 Wh/kg cell-level energy density while enhancing safety and supply-chain resilience.

Key Investment Highlights

-Targeting the $50B+ dual-use UAS battery market with strong long-term growth trends;

-Proprietary NAEPE electrolyte chemistry and UV Snap-Cure heritage supported by 1,500+ global patents and R&D 100 Award-winning technology;

-Pathway toward Blue UAS approval supported by ITRI's role in Green UAS certification;

-Phase 1 establishment of a 100MWh automated production line in Baytown, Texas, with multi-phase expansion plans; and

-Leadership team with 30+ years of global manufacturing, OEM sales, and capital markets experience.

The Baytown facility will include an ISO-7 certified ultra-dry room environment capable of maintaining a dew point of <=-40C. The production line will incorporate customized automation, including vacuum die-coating systems, solid-electrolyte molding, automated stacking, and pouch-sealing modules. The project is designed to transition from pilot-scale validation to a fully operational domestic manufacturing asset capable of supplying high-density power solutions to the UAV industry.

According to Research and Markets, the UAV battery market is expected to grow from $6.01 billion in 2024 to $15.31 billion by 2029, driven by rising defense demand, tactical drone logistics, and the need for U.S. sovereign battery solutions.

J-Star is a holding company with operations conducted through subsidiaries in Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Samoa with its headquarters in Taiwan.

More information and complete details:

starholding.com

ymacorp.com

Fri Apr 3, 2026: Quanta Services Expands Portfolio with Tri-City Group Acquisition

Quanta Services, Inc. has completed the acquisition of the Davenport-based Tri-City Group, a specialized contractor known for its expertise in electrical construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th April 2026, further solidifying Quanta's position as a leader in providing integrated engineering and construction services for electric power and renewable energy sectors.

In a statement from Lori Rochholz, director of marketing for Tri-City Group, it was emphasized that the company will retain its name and brand identity post-acquisition. 'Tri-City Group will continue to operate as Tri-City Group. Our name is not changing. Our brand is not changing. The Palmer Family and the leadership team that has guided this company will continue to lead it,' she noted, assuring customers that they will experience the same quality and responsiveness as before, now enhanced by Quanta Services' extensive resources.

Quanta Services, headquartered in Houston, Texas, also announced other acquisitions alongside Tri-City Group, including Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service. Duke Austin, president and CEO of Quanta Services, stated that these acquisitions broaden their craft-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs across the United States. With approximately 69,000 employees, Quanta Services is poised to leverage its expanded capabilities to meet growing customer demand in the infrastructure sector.

The Tri-City Group, which rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diverse range of services, has a long-standing presence in the Quad Cities since 1895. It encompasses various subsidiaries, including Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, and has contributed to projects across 46 states and several countries. The company remains committed to investing in its local community, as highlighted in their recent statement, reaffirming their dedication to the region and its workforce.

This acquisition not only enhances Quanta Services' operational footprint but also signifies a strategic move towards reinforcing its leadership in the electrical infrastructure market. As the company continues to expand its portfolio, the integration of Tri-City Group is expected to bring significant synergies, solidifying its competitive edge in the industry.

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-G7C3-SFSX-M1CP-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

value.Title: J-Star Holding Joins Forces with Patriot Green Energy Technology for Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-XS03-S5K1-82RB-00000-00J-Star Holding Joins Forces with Patriot Green Energy Technology for Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisJ-Star Holding Joins Forces with Patriot Green Energy Technology for Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions

J-Star Holding Co., Ltd., reported it has formed a strategic partnership with Patriot Green Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (PSSB), an industry developer and manufacturer of next-generation solid state battery technology for high-performance and defense applications with a large minority ownership by Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI), to develop Made-in-USA polymer-based solid-state battery solutions.

The company said the collaboration includes plans to establish a 100 megawatt-hour (MWh) modular, automated solid-state battery production line in Baytown, Texas to support the growing requirements of the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) and drone sector.

In a release, J-Star Holding noted partnership details and highlights include:

J-Star and PSSB have jointly submitted a grant application to the U.S. Department of Energy to support the development of the facility. The project has also received a commitment letter from the Baytown Economic Development Foundation securing a parcel in the TGS Cedar Port Industrial Park for construction of the factory.

The initiative is aligned with the U.S.-Taiwan Agreement on Research and Technology (ART) framework, which provides a bilateral pathway for technical cooperation and outward investment clearances from Taiwan's Ministry of Economic Affairs (MOEA). In January 2026, the United States and Taiwan signed a landmark trade and investment agreement with Taiwanese semiconductor and technology companies committing $250 billion in new direct investment into the United States and $250 billion in credit guarantee by the government of Taiwan to support additional investment, strengthening collaboration in semiconductor supply chains, industrial parks, artificial intelligence, energy, and defense technologies.

Jonathan Chiang, CEO and Chairman of J-Star Holding, commented, "We continue to strengthen our relationship with PSSB with this new 'Made-In-USA' initiative to develop a fully automated next-generation solid-state batteries production line in Texas. This partnership marks a major step in building a secure, friend-shored supply chain infrastructure and solutions in the United States. With strong government support and growing demand from aerospace and defense customers, we believe this positions J-Star at the forefront of advance battery manufacturing."

Cheng Zhang Lu (Peter Lu), Chairman & CEO of PSSB, has committed to transferring the company's full technical blueprint to the Baytown facility, including extended on-site technical residency, local hiring, and workforce training to support long-term operations. This partnership combines ITRI-backed innovation with U.S. manufacturing capabilities to accelerate the commercialization of polymer-based solid-state batteries and help establish a domestic production ecosystem for the rapidly growing UAV and drone markets.

Strategic Partnership and Market Opportunity

Through YMA (TX) INC., J-Star is leveraging PSSB's venture backing from ITRI to pursue supply agreements with Tier-1 U.S. aerospace and defense OEMs. The partnership is designed to accelerate qualification with U.S. federal contractors by leveraging technology that has already undergone validation in Taiwan.

The project aims to establish a U.S. domestic, fully automated production line for high-density polymer-based solid-state batteries supporting the rapidly expanding UAV and drone markets. The facility is expected to scale manufacturing to 100 MWh annual nameplate capacity, targeting over 350 Wh/kg cell-level energy density while enhancing safety and supply-chain resilience.

Key Investment Highlights

-Targeting the $50B+ dual-use UAS battery market with strong long-term growth trends;

-Proprietary NAEPE electrolyte chemistry and UV Snap-Cure heritage supported by 1,500+ global patents and R&D 100 Award-winning technology;

-Pathway toward Blue UAS approval supported by ITRI's role in Green UAS certification;

-Phase 1 establishment of a 100MWh automated production line in Baytown, Texas, with multi-phase expansion plans; and

-Leadership team with 30+ years of global manufacturing, OEM sales, and capital markets experience.

The Baytown facility will include an ISO-7 certified ultra-dry room environment capable of maintaining a dew point of <=-40C. The production line will incorporate customized automation, including vacuum die-coating systems, solid-electrolyte molding, automated stacking, and pouch-sealing modules. The project is designed to transition from pilot-scale validation to a fully operational domestic manufacturing asset capable of supplying high-density power solutions to the UAV industry.

According to Research and Markets, the UAV battery market is expected to grow from $6.01 billion in 2024 to $15.31 billion by 2029, driven by rising defense demand, tactical drone logistics, and the need for U.S. sovereign battery solutions.

J-Star is a holding company with operations conducted through subsidiaries in Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Samoa with its headquarters in Taiwan.

More information and complete details:

starholding.com

ymacorp.com

((Comments on this story may be sent to newsdesk@closeupmedia.com))

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT9J-208H-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives.
