value.Title: M . A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGG-8H13-RRXY-F1K7-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ U.S. ARMY CORPS FUNDS $610M FACILITY TO PRODUCE STERILE SCREWWORMS; MORTENSON TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION

M A Mortenson Company has won a $610 million contract from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to construct the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas. The bid supports ongoing efforts to prevent the New World screwworm from spreading into the United States from Mexico, where authorities report rising activity near the Texas border. As of March 4, 2026, there were 989 active cases nationwide, including 19 in Tamaulipas, a rise from 11 weeks prior, underscoring the need for robust control measures such as the deployment of sterilized flies along the border. Mortenson’s facility will play a pivotal role in ensuring a reliable supply of sterile insects, reinforcing the USDA’s efforts to protect livestock and sustain farm income in border states.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SHAPES RESILIENCE-DRIVEN URBAN RENEWAL IN SOUTH FLORIDA

On the 28th of March 2026, an industry roundtable hosted with the South Florida Business Journal gathered leaders from construction, development, and public-private sectors to discuss the rapid skyline transformation unfolding across the region and DPR Construction’s role in it.Collaboration and integrated project delivery were highlighted as essential in South Florida’s crowded, high stakes market. By aligning stakeholders early, teams can achieve faster schedules, safer sites, and higher quality, while navigating limited land and rising costs through more thoughtful, resilient design.Innovation and technology were identified as key accelerants. DPR Construction and partners are advancing prefabrication, modular approaches, and data driven planning to shorten timelines and improve accuracy. The emphasis on digital tools supports better forecasting and coordination across multi phase efforts, with safety and sustainability guiding decision making.With growth comes the need for a skilled workforce, educational partnerships, and robust training pipelines to sustain momentum. The conversation also underscored the importance of building climate resilient infrastructure and community centered design to ensure new developments endure.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO HEAD OF CAPITAL STRATEGY

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, based in the firm's New York City office.He will refine and execute the funding strategy, reporting to president and CEO James Patchett, and will help guide capital strategy and investment decisions while building relationships with funding and equity partners.Vlahakis previously served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he led equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities highlights his breadth of financial leadership.Gilbane says the appointment strengthens its capital program and supports its growth plans in core sectors.

+ GILBANE-WKU PPP AIMS TO MODERNIZE HOUSING, EXPAND STUDENT RESOURCES

WKU announced the approval of a public-private partnership with Gilbane to develop a new residence hall replacing Keen and Poland Halls, with the Board of Regents' approval on 11th December 2025. The Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026 clears the way for construction. SGA Senator Barker stated the partnership is fully authorized and that WKU is now ready to begin building. The SGA also advanced measures to benefit students, including Resolution 7-26-S for a crosswalk near housing and collaboration with the Kentucky DOT, and Bill 17-26-S providing $50 reimbursements for textbooks, along with provisions for Scantrons and lab goggles.

+ DC AFFORDABLE HOUSING EXPANSION: GILBANE STARTS LEASING FOR 229-UNIT PHASE II

Gilbane Development has initiated leasing for Phase II of its Barnaby & 7th redevelopment in Southeast Washington, D.C., delivering 229 units across four new buildings, The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis. As of 3rd April 2026, the project is on track for completion by summer 2026 and features a mix of studios through three-bedroom units serving households at 50% and 80% of AMI, with 20% of units set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing under DC DHS.Phase I, The Upland, was completed in Spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, including 34 PSH units. Income restrictions for Phase I range from 30% to 50% of AMI.Phase III is expected to add about 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the grand total to 498 units upon completion. A pedestrian walkway now connects all six buildings in the larger development, and DC Housing Finance Agency places Phase II's cost at roughly $135 million.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

+ VPM AND CLARK CONSTRUCTION OPEN NEW HEADQUARTERS TO NEXT-GENERATION BUILDERS

VPM hosted students from the Metropolitan Business League at its new headquarters, which is under construction by Clark Construction. The event, part of a 'Youth in Construction' initiative, occurred on 19th November 2025 and gave Richmond Technical Center students an inside view of trades engaged in major projects, including carpentry, electrical work, HVAC, and plumbing. The session also featured Baker Concrete's apprenticeship program, illustrating the numerous career options within the industry. This immersive experience allowed students to imagine themselves contributing to similar developments in the future. VPM began work on its new headquarters in August 2024, with completion slated for 2026. The Monroe Ward relocation is intended to centralize VPM’s news and content production, increasing accessibility for the public. By expanding its presence in the Richmond area, VPM aims to strengthen coverage of local news and events. In addition to trade demonstrations, students had the chance to engage with VPM's broadcasting capabilities, recording clips in the mobile sound booth to gain firsthand understanding of how the organization delivers news to millions through TV and radio channels. Community support remains a key element for VPM, enabling ongoing local news coverage, educational programming, and inspiring content. As VPM progresses its development with Clark Construction, the collaboration exemplifies how community-oriented initiatives can enrich local education and industry awareness.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Fri Apr 3, 2026: Quanta Services Expands Portfolio with Tri-City Group Acquisition

Quanta Services, Inc. has completed the acquisition of the Davenport-based Tri-City Group, a specialized contractor known for its expertise in electrical construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th April 2026, further solidifying Quanta's position as a leader in providing integrated engineering and construction services for electric power and renewable energy sectors.

In a statement from Lori Rochholz, director of marketing for Tri-City Group, it was emphasized that the company will retain its name and brand identity post-acquisition. 'Tri-City Group will continue to operate as Tri-City Group. Our name is not changing. Our brand is not changing. The Palmer Family and the leadership team that has guided this company will continue to lead it,' she noted, assuring customers that they will experience the same quality and responsiveness as before, now enhanced by Quanta Services' extensive resources.

Quanta Services, headquartered in Houston, Texas, also announced other acquisitions alongside Tri-City Group, including Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service. Duke Austin, president and CEO of Quanta Services, stated that these acquisitions broaden their craft-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs across the United States. With approximately 69,000 employees, Quanta Services is poised to leverage its expanded capabilities to meet growing customer demand in the infrastructure sector.

The Tri-City Group, which rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diverse range of services, has a long-standing presence in the Quad Cities since 1895. It encompasses various subsidiaries, including Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, and has contributed to projects across 46 states and several countries. The company remains committed to investing in its local community, as highlighted in their recent statement, reaffirming their dedication to the region and its workforce.

This acquisition not only enhances Quanta Services' operational footprint but also signifies a strategic move towards reinforcing its leadership in the electrical infrastructure market. As the company continues to expand its portfolio, the integration of Tri-City Group is expected to bring significant synergies, solidifying its competitive edge in the industry.

Fri Apr 3, 2026: Quanta Services Expands Portfolio with Tri-City Group Acquisition

Quanta Services, Inc. has completed the acquisition of the Davenport-based Tri-City Group, a specialized contractor known for its expertise in electrical construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th April 2026, further solidifying Quanta's position as a leader in providing integrated engineering and construction services for electric power and renewable energy sectors.

In a statement from Lori Rochholz, director of marketing for Tri-City Group, it was emphasized that the company will retain its name and brand identity post-acquisition. 'Tri-City Group will continue to operate as Tri-City Group. Our name is not changing. Our brand is not changing. The Palmer Family and the leadership team that has guided this company will continue to lead it,' she noted, assuring customers that they will experience the same quality and responsiveness as before, now enhanced by Quanta Services' extensive resources.

Quanta Services, headquartered in Houston, Texas, also announced other acquisitions alongside Tri-City Group, including Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service. Duke Austin, president and CEO of Quanta Services, stated that these acquisitions broaden their craft-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs across the United States. With approximately 69,000 employees, Quanta Services is poised to leverage its expanded capabilities to meet growing customer demand in the infrastructure sector.

The Tri-City Group, which rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diverse range of services, has a long-standing presence in the Quad Cities since 1895. It encompasses various subsidiaries, including Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, and has contributed to projects across 46 states and several countries. The company remains committed to investing in its local community, as highlighted in their recent statement, reaffirming their dedication to the region and its workforce.

This acquisition not only enhances Quanta Services' operational footprint but also signifies a strategic move towards reinforcing its leadership in the electrical infrastructure market. As the company continues to expand its portfolio, the integration of Tri-City Group is expected to bring significant synergies, solidifying its competitive edge in the industry.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.56 and positive MACD:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Quanta Services, Inc. is 1.56. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. It is higher than the Price/MAP 200 for the S&P 500 Index of 1.07, a second bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $579.35 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $466.25, a third bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $466.25, a fourth bullish indicator.

- The Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator of 12-day Exponential Moving Average (EMA) of 631.84 minus the 26-day EMA of 605.04 is positive, suggesting a bullish signal. Both the 12-day EMA as well as the 26-day EMA are rising, another bullish signal.

- The price to 50-day EMAP ratio is 1.3, a bullish indicator. The 50-day EMAP has increased to $577.0. An increase is another bullish indicator.

Past Month:

- Rises to Falls: In the past month the number of rises outnumbered falls 15:8 or 1.9:1.

- Quanta Services, Inc. stock was the tenth highest performer in the S&P 500 Index.

- Quanta Services, Inc. stock was the fourteenth highest performer in the NYSE market.

Past Quarter:

In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twenty-four times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

Relative Strength (6 months) 94 percentile:

- A price rise combined with a high relative strength is a bullish signal.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R3X4-00000-00W rtsil Secures Landmark 790MW Off-Grid Power Order in Texas2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisW rtsil Secures Landmark 790MW Off-Grid Power Order in Texas

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 07:30 EDT

Finnish technology group Wärtsilä has announced a significant milestone with the acquisition of a 790MW off-grid power solution contract for a new data centre in Texas. This development marks Wärtsilä's fifth data centre-related order in the United States and its inaugural project in the state of Texas. The announcement was made on the 30th of April 2026, highlighting the growing importance of reliable energy solutions in the data centre sector.

The Texas facility will be powered by 42 Wärtsilä 50SG engines that run on natural gas. The delivery of the necessary equipment is scheduled for 2028, with the power plant expected to commence full operations by late 2029. This strategic move is aimed at addressing the urgent energy requirements of the region, which is becoming a crucial hub for artificial intelligence (AI) model training and large-scale infrastructure developments.

Wärtsilä's engines are specifically designed to operate efficiently under Texas's high-temperature conditions, ensuring optimal performance regardless of the heat. The average heat rate of the engines is 6,800 British thermal units per kilowatt-hour, a specification that Wärtsilä believes will help manage both fuel costs and emissions levels effectively. This focus on efficiency is expected to streamline the environmental and regulatory approval processes for the installation.

Moreover, the data centre's dedicated off-grid power plant is anticipated to facilitate future integration with renewable energy sources. Once connected to the grid, the facility will have the capability to support balancing functions for renewable power in the area, further underscoring Wärtsilä's commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

As Texas continues to face challenges in meeting its growing power demands, Wärtsilä's off-grid solution presents a viable alternative to help ensure uninterrupted energy supply for critical operations at the data centre. This contract not only reinforces Wärtsilä's position in the North American market but also highlights the company's focus on innovative energy solutions tailored to meet the specific needs of evolving industries.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 WARTSILA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Wartsila Activities

Activities

Wärtsilä, a Finnish engineering company, is a global leader in innovative technologies for the marine and energy sectors. Its core business activities encompass the design, manufacturing, and servicing of engines, propulsion systems, and power plants, with a strong emphasis on sustainable solutions such as gas-fueled and hybrid technologies. The company provides comprehensive lifecycle services, including maintenance, upgrades, and digital optimization tools to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are energy storage systems and digital services, driven by the increasing demand for renewable integration and decarbonization in the power sector. Additionally, Wärtsilä's marine operations are expanding through advanced fuel-flexible engines and smart vessel management systems, supporting the global shift toward greener shipping. This strategic focus positions Wärtsilä as a key player in addressing the challenges of energy transition and operational reliability. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 5th largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-4YP3-S2GT-82YY-00000-00Citroen Could Release An EV Costing Less Than $20,0772026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisCitroen Could Release An EV Costing Less Than $20,077

Citroen may launch an electric vehicle priced under $20,077 if European regulators approve a new vehicle classification. The French automaker could introduce a successor to the C1 that echoes the iconic 2CV's character while looking completely different, according to Autocar reports. This would position Citroen among Europe's most affordable electric options.For entities like Ferrari N.V. (NYSE: RACE) that focus on the...

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY UPDATE: THE RECENT NEWS OF KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-M1R3-RT73-61SX-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY UPDATE: THE RECENT NEWS OF KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - INDUSTRY UPDATE: THE RECENT NEWS OF KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: J-Star Holding Partners With Patriot Green Energy Technology For Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-03H3-SCMW-X081-00000-00J-Star Holding Partners With Patriot Green Energy Technology For Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisJ-Star Holding Partners With Patriot Green Energy Technology For Made-In-USA Solid-State Battery Solutions

Establishment of 100MWh Modular Automated Solid-State Battery Production Line in TexasTAICHUNG CITY, Taiwan, May 01, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- J-Star Holding Co., Ltd. (Nasdaq: YMAT) (“J-Star” or the“Company”), today announced it has formed a strategic partnership with Patriot Green Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (“PSSB”), a leading industry developer and manufacturer of next-generation solid state battery technology for high-performance and defense applications with a large minority ownership by Industrial Technology Research Institute (“ITRI”), to develop Made-in-USA polymer-based solid-state battery solutions. The collaboration includes plans to establish a 100 megawatt-hour (“MWh”) modular, automated solid-state battery production line in Baytown, Texas to support the growing requirements of the unmanned aerial vehicle (“UAV”) and drone sector.


J-Star and PSSB have jointly submitted a grant application to the U.S. Department of Energy to support the development of the facility. The project has also received a commitment letter from the Baytown Economic Development Foundation securing a parcel in the TGS Cedar Port Industrial Park for construction of the factory.
The initiative is aligned with the U.S.–Taiwan Agreement on Research and Technology (ART) framework, which provides a bilateral pathway for technical cooperation and outward investment clearances from Taiwan's Ministry of Economic Affairs (“MOEA”). In January 2026, the United States and Taiwan signed a landmark trade and investment agreement with Taiwanese semiconductor and technology companies committing $250 billion in new direct investment into the United States and $250 billion in credit guarantee by the government of Taiwan to support additional investment, strengthening collaboration in semiconductor supply chains, industrial parks, artificial intelligence, energy, and defense technologies.
Jonathan Chiang, CEO and Chairman of J-Star Holding, commented,“We continue to strengthen our relationship with PSSB with this new 'Made-In-USA' initiative to develop a fully automated next-generation solid-state batteries production line in Texas. This partnership marks a major step in building a secure, friend-shored supply chain infrastructure and solutions in the United States. With strong government support and growing demand from aerospace and defense customers, we believe this positions J-Star at the forefront of advance battery manufacturing.”
Cheng Zhang Lu (Peter Lu), Chairman & CEO of PSSB, has committed to transferring the company's full technical blueprint to the Baytown facility, including extended on-site technical residency, local hiring, and workforce training to support long-term operations. This partnership combines ITRI-backed innovation with U.S. manufacturing capabilities to accelerate the commercialization of polymer-based solid-state batteries and help establish a domestic production ecosystem for the rapidly growing UAV and drone markets.
Strategic Partnership and Market Opportunity
Through YMA (TX) INC., J-Star is leveraging PSSB's venture backing from ITRI to pursue supply agreements with Tier-1 U.S. aerospace and defense OEMs. The partnership is designed to accelerate qualification with U.S. federal contractors by leveraging technology that has already undergone validation in Taiwan.
The project aims to establish a U.S. domestic, fully automated production line for high-density polymer-based solid-state batteries supporting the rapidly expanding UAV and drone markets. The facility is expected to scale manufacturing to 100 MWh annual nameplate capacity, targeting over 350 Wh/kg cell-level energy density while enhancing safety and supply-chain resilience.
Key Investment Highlights

Targeting the $50B+ dual-use UAS battery market with strong long-term growth trends;

Proprietary NAEPE electrolyte chemistry and UV Snap-Cure heritage supported by 1,500+ global patents and R&D 100 Award-winning technology;

Pathway toward Blue UAS approval supported by ITRI's role in Green UAS certification;

Phase 1 establishment of a 100MWh automated production line in Baytown, Texas, with multi-phase expansion plans; and

Leadership team with 30+ years of global manufacturing, OEM sales, and capital markets experience.

The Baytown facility will include an ISO-7 certified ultra-dry room environment capable of maintaining a dew point of ≤-40°C. The production line will incorporate customized automation, including vacuum die-coating systems, solid-electrolyte molding, automated stacking, and pouch-sealing modules. The project is designed to transition from pilot-scale validation to a fully operational domestic manufacturing asset capable of supplying high-density power solutions to the UAV industry.
According to Research and Markets, the UAV battery market is expected to grow from $6.01 billion in 2024 to $15.31 billion by 2029, driven by rising defense demand, tactical drone logistics, and the need for U.S. sovereign battery solutions.
About J-Star Holding Co., Ltd.
J-Star (NASDAQ: YMAT) is a holding company with operations conducted through subsidiaries in Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Samoa with its headquarters in Taiwan. J-Star's predecessor group was established in 1970, and has accumulated over 50 years of know-how in material composites industry. J-Star develops and commercializes the technology on carbon reinforcement and resin systems. With decades of experience and knowledge in composites and materials, J-Star is able to apply its expertise and technology to design and manufacture a great variety of lightweight, high-performance carbon composite products, ranging from key structural parts of electric bicycles and sports bicycles, rackets, automobile parts to healthcare products. Visit j-starholding and to learn more.
Forward Looking-Statements
Certain statements contained in this press release about future expectations, plans and prospects, as well as any other statements regarding matters that are not historical facts, may constitute“forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements include, but are not limited to, statements relating to the expected trading commencement and closing dates. The words“anticipate,”“believe,”“continue,”“could,”“estimate,”“expect,”“intend,”“may,”“plan,”“potential,”“predict,”“project,”“should,”“target,”“will,”“would” and similar expressions are intended to identify forward-looking statements, although not all forward-looking statements contain these identifying words. Actual results may differ materially from those indicated by such forward-looking statements as a result of various important factors, including: the uncertainties related to market conditions and other factors discussed in the“Risk Factors” section of the final prospectus filed with the SEC. For these reasons, among others, investors are cautioned not to place undue reliance upon any forward-looking statements in this press release. Any forward-looking statements contained in this press release speak only as of the date hereof, and J-Star specifically disclaims any obligation to update any forward-looking statement, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.

Used electric vehicle sales are climbing sharplyas prices close in on those of comparable gas-powered models. A key factor behind the supply surge is the volume of vehicles leased between late 2022 and the end of 2023. Most of those agreements run three years, and the cars are now flowing back onto dealer lots in large numbers.

value.Title: Cromarty Green Hydrogen Project's future secured after being acquired by Protium in £100m investment; the project is located not far from the Cromarty Firth near the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm in Easter Ross
value.Overview: Inverness Courier, MERGERS AND ACQUISITIONS NEWS & ENVIRONMENT NEWS, 611words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-Y5W3-RXF7-C09R-00000-00Cromarty Green Hydrogen Project's future secured after being acquired by Protium in £100m investment; the project is located not far from the Cromarty Firth near the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm in Easter Ross2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisMERGERS AND ACQUISITIONS NEWS & ENVIRONMENT NEWSCromarty Green Hydrogen Project's future secured after being acquired by Protium in £100m investment; the project is located not far from the Cromarty Firth near the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm in Easter Rossphilip.murray@hnmedia.co.uk (Philip Murray)

A major green energy scheme in the Highlands has "secured its future" after Protium acquired the from Storegga as part of a £100m investment.

The 15MW project, located east of the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm not far from the B9176 Struie road, is aimed at decarbonising Highland industries, , while supporting local jobs and introducing a new community benefit fund tied to production capacity.

The green hydrogen project had been a , but questions were raised about its future last autumn when Scottish Power paused its UK green hydrogen plans.

But the take-over of the project from Storegga - as well as Storegga's wider Scottish development portfolio - has secured its immediate future, with Protium saying the move will help ensure the development remains rooted in Scotland.

"We are proud to become the custodians of the Cromarty project," said Christopher Jackson, CEO of Protium. "Our focus is energy certainty. By localising production, we ensure that the Highlands' world-class industry and transport sectors aren't just surviving the Net Zero transition, they are leading it".

Protium already operates the UK's first and largest commercial green hydrogen plant, Pioneer 1, in Wales.

Building on that momentum, Protium's Pioneer 2 flagship facility (2.5MW) has recently achieved "first gas" and is now in its commissioning phase. Once fully operational in June, Pioneer 2 will scale production to one tonne of low-carbon hydrogen per day. Protium project this will abate approximately 3600 tonnes of CO2 annually - the environmental equivalent of removing 2,200 cars from UK roads every year.

Beyond Wales and Scotland, Protium is also progressing projects in South Tees.

Those behind the Cromarty project claim it will produce approximately 2000 tonnes per annum.

With offices in London, Port Talbot and Edinburgh, the green hydrogen company says it is committed to creating high-skilled jobs and apprenticeships within the Highland Council area and beyond.

Those behind the Cromarty Hydrogen Project , and more to follow as it develops further.

Protium views itself as a long-term partner in the Highland economy and said it is committed to an open dialogue with local communities, which it claims will ensure insights and feedback inform how the 15MW electrolyser plans are designed and delivered.

The Cromarty project was a cornerstone of the UK government's Hydrogen Allocation Round 1 (HAR1), administered by the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero.

Mr Jackson says Protium's Wales developments, acquisition of the Cromarty project and plans for Teeside will boost UK energy security at a crucial time.

"This acquisition materially strengthens our national platform and bolsters UK energy security by anchoring production within our own borders," he said.

"Protium is building permanent, industrial-scale green hydrogen infrastructure that decarbonises British industry today. With operational assets in Wales, presence in Scotland, and projects advancing in South Tees, we are scaling toward a 1GW UK portfolio to establish a resilient, self-sufficient, and competitive domestic hydrogen economy."

Welcoming the acquisition of the Cromarty project, Tim Stedman, CEO of Storegga, added: "Over recent years, Storegga has established a strong portfolio of green hydrogen opportunities in Scotland.

"Our priority has been to ensure these projects move forward under an established developer, with the platform and capability to take them into delivery.

"Protium's track record and growing UK presence position them well to advance these projects, and we look forward to seeing their full potential realised to support a more sustainable industrial future in Scotland."

An artist's impression of the Cromarty project's design, subject to detailed development, technical assessments, and planning approvals.

urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R3BC-00000-00Sempra (NYSE:SRE) Sells 45% Infrastructure Stake to KKR for $10 Billion2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:45ZLexisNexisSempra (NYSE:SRE) Sells 45% Infrastructure Stake to KKR for $10 Billion

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

12 May 2026 14:15 EDT

On 10th May 2026, Sempra (NYSE:SRE) divests 45% of its infrastructure unit to private equity firm KKR for $10,000,000,000, a move designed to simplify its business and bolster its balance sheet. The infrastructure unit being sold includes LNG terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra (NYSE:SRE) Chairman and CEO, notes the transaction will streamline operations and fund growth in key U.S. markets, signaling a long-term partnership with KKR to advance LNG exports.

Upon closing, a KKR-led consortium will hold 65% of Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra retains 25% and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority takes the remaining 10%. The deal positions roughly 95% of Sempra's earnings to come from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This divestiture redirects Sempra's focus toward its regulated utilities, enabling increased investments in core operations and growth initiatives in states such as Texas and California. Analysts will monitor how the arrangement affects long-term growth and market position, while investors welcomed the announcement as shares rose nearly 5% on the news

SECTION 1 SEMPRA RECENT PRESS RELEASES

March 02: Sempra quarterly dividends up 1.9% sequentially from previous quarter

Sempra today announced a quarterly dividend of 65.75c per share for Q4/2025; up 1.9% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 64.50c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15.

March 02: Sempra quarterly dividends up 1.9% sequentially from previous quarter

Sempra today announced a quarterly dividend of 65.75c per share for Q4/2025; up 1.9% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 64.50c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15.

November 10, 2025: Sempra quarterly dividends steady

Sempra today announced a quarterly dividend of 64.50c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 64.50c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, December 11 and the record date is Thursday, December 11, 2025 and it is payable on Thursday, January 15, 2026.

SECTION 2 SEMPRA ACTIVITIES

Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W261-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE PROJECT IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation is making its entry into the Oklahoma market by securing a contract for the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement project. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry Group in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver this significant infrastructure upgrade. This partnership marks the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the Oklahoma DOT, highlighting the company's ambition to expand its regional presence.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, signifies a major investment in modernizing a critical transportation corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has been operational since 1945 and serves as an essential link for local communities, businesses, and visitors traveling to the Lake Texoma area. With the new bridge, Zachry Construction aims to enhance mobility, safety, and economic activity in southeastern Oklahoma.Zachry and Traylor Bros. will construct a new four-lane bridge that includes modern features such as bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also entails the demolition of the existing bridge, with traffic being maintained on it during the construction phase to minimize disruption to the traveling public. Echoing the significance of this venture, Travis Mross, president of Zachry Construction, stated, 'This project is an important milestone for Zachry, as it strengthens a vital connection for the communities around Lake Texoma.'The project is designed to improve safety, reliability, and resilience for the future. It aims to address the increasing traffic demands in the area while ensuring that local commerce and tourism continue to flourish. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has set a completion target of 1st October 2029 for the Roosevelt Bridge replacement project, which is expected to elevate the standard of infrastructure in the region.Zachry Construction Corporation, a third-generation family business with over a century of experience, will leverage its extensive expertise in heavy and highway construction for this initiative. The joint venture with Traylor Bros., known for its proficiency in complex bridge projects, promises to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and maintains mobility throughout construction. Together, they are poised to provide lasting value to the state and its communities.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORP. ELEVATES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AMID STRATEGIC GROWTH

Zachry Construction Corp. has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, marking a significant transition in leadership for the San Antonio-based company. Mross, who began his career with the family-owned business as an intern in 2000, officially took on the presidential role on 29th April 2026.Over the years, Mross has steadily advanced through the ranks, culminating in his previous position as executive vice president for the past six years. In this role, he played a crucial part in shaping Zachry Construction's strategic direction and enhancing operational efficiency. He succeeds David Zachry, who will now concentrate solely on his responsibilities as CEO."Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," David Zachry stated. He emphasized Mross's deep understanding of the business and his contributions to instilling discipline and accountability, which are essential for the company's continued success. Under Mross's leadership, Zachry Construction aims to maintain its momentum and explore new opportunities.Zachry Construction Corp. is recognized for its extensive work in heavy civil, roadway, and infrastructure projects, with over 6,000 completed initiatives in both public and private sectors. The company is currently expanding its operations in the water and wastewater infrastructure domain, considered one of the fastest-growing segments in the construction industry. Recent projects include water storage and dams for various authorities, and the acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. has further strengthened its capabilities in this area.As president, Mross will oversee operations across multiple sectors, including water, transportation, underground utilities, and civil sitework. His priorities will focus on strategic growth, operational excellence, and nurturing a robust corporate culture while maintaining a gritty mindset. Mross stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW LEASE NEAR LA VILLITA

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL AND NABTU JOIN FORCES TO ENHANCE NUCLEAR CONSTRUCTION TRAINING

Bechtel and North America's Building Trades Unions (NABTU) have announced a new partnership aimed at modernizing apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction. This collaboration comes as the U.S. seeks to bolster its nuclear infrastructure, particularly with the rise of large-scale reactors and Small Modular Reactors (SMRs). The Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) was signed on the 5th of May, 2026, marking a significant step in workforce development for the nuclear sector.The strategic agreement highlights the alignment between Bechtel, a leader in nuclear infrastructure construction, and NABTU, which is the largest federation of skilled construction unions in North America. The partnership aims to enhance apprenticeship pathways and expand specialized training that focuses on the technical requirements and quality standards necessary for modern nuclear projects. This initiative is especially critical as the industry prepares for increased demand in nuclear energy solutions.Under the terms of the MOU, Bechtel and NABTU will work collaboratively to identify the specialized craft skills needed for nuclear construction, ensuring that apprenticeship training is appropriately aligned. The organizations plan to modernize curricula at union training centers that support cutting-edge nuclear construction methods and technologies. Additionally, they will coordinate recruitment efforts through union networks to create more pathways into high-skill careers in building large reactors and SMRs.Brendan Bechtel, Chair and CEO of Bechtel, emphasized the importance of this partnership in delivering nuclear power plants that require exceptional craftsmanship and a commitment to safety. He noted that the collaboration builds upon their successful history with NABTU, including projects such as the Vogtle Units 3 and 4, which are the first new nuclear reactors constructed in the U.S. in over 30 years. Bechtel’s current initiatives, including the Natrium Demonstration Project, reflect a forward-looking approach toward nuclear innovation.NABTU President Sean McGarvey echoed this sentiment, stating that the construction of the next generation of nuclear projects demands high levels of skill and precision, starting with robust apprenticeship training. The agreement aims to ensure a steady pipeline of job-ready craft professionals, alleviating potential labor shortages for upcoming projects. As the U.S. forges ahead with nuclear energy advancements, this partnership between Bechtel and NABTU will play a crucial role in developing the skilled workforce necessary to meet future energy demands.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ANNOUNCED TO BOOST MINING PROJECTS IN CHILE

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverr a Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. This collaboration aims to effectively support the delivery of significant mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026.As investments in mining continue to surge in the region, the demand for efficient project execution that adheres to rigorous safety and quality standards has never been higher. The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies; the engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA’s established reputation for direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Lucy Martin, President of the Mining & Metals division of the global firm, emphasized the necessity for a refined execution model in delivering complex projects at scale. She stated, "This partnership reflects a deliberate approach to execution, combining our end-to-end delivery capability with EIMISA’s strong construction presence in Chile to strengthen how we deliver across the region."Dar o Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, echoed this sentiment, asserting that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies. He noted, "Bechtel’s global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile’s growing portfolio of complex projects."With a history of over 400 completed projects and a workforce exceeding 8,000, EIMISA has positioned itself as a vital partner in Latin America’s construction sector. The collaboration with the global engineering firm is poised to enhance the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects, contributing to the region's economic growth and development.

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO ENHANCE PIPEFITTING SKILLS IN LAKE CHARLES

Bechtel, a leading global engineering and construction firm, has announced a partnership with SOWELA Technical Community College to introduce a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA’s Main Campus in Lake Charles. This initiative aims to equip local students with the essential skills needed for a career in pipefitting, a critical trade in various industries including manufacturing and power generation. The program is set to commence on 26th May 2026 and will run until 13th November 2026.The training program is designed to provide students with a comprehensive introduction to the pipefitting trade, covering core job responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used in the field. Participants will engage in hands-on learning, focusing on the installation and repair of high- and low-pressure pipe systems, which are vital to the functioning of manufacturing facilities, power generation plants, and building heating and cooling systems.Classes will be held Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., allowing participants to gain a thorough understanding of the pipefitting profession over the course of the program. Upon successful completion, students will earn an OSHA 10-hour safety credential and will be well-prepared to pursue the National Center for Construction Education and Research (NCCER) Pipefitting certifications, enhancing their employability in a competitive job market.To qualify for employment consideration, successful program completers must pass a background check and drug screening, ensuring that they meet industry standards. Interested individuals can find more information or register for the program by visiting www.sowela.edu/pipefitting. For further inquiries, SOWELA’s Office of Workforce Solutions can be contacted at (337) 421-6560 or via email at workforce@sowela.edu.This collaboration between Bechtel and SOWELA highlights the commitment of both organizations to workforce development in the Lake Charles area, addressing the growing demand for skilled labor in the construction and manufacturing sectors. By providing accessible training opportunities, the program aims to empower local residents and contribute to the region 's economic growth.
